cause_analysis.narrative: DNAILS | Russian Nails churned on 2026-02-10 after 175 days with Zoca. They sent an explicit cancellation request on 2026-01-27 via phone_call — 14 days before churn. Classification: explicit cancellation. The client formally asked to cancel; the question is whether a save attempt was made. 3 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days — this is the clearest operational failure on Zoca's side.
recommendations: [Retention] Cancellation was signalled 14 day(s) before churn via phone_call. Trigger an AM save call within 4 hours of any cancellation keyword — not a support response.
[Offer] Build a 3-tier retention offer ladder (pause / discount / scope change) so AMs have something concrete to counter with.
[SLA] 3 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days. Enforce a hard rule: every inbound gets a human response within 24h, every day of the week.
[SLA example] On 2025-12-25 via app_chat, client wrote: "Yes…" — no outbound response in 18 days.
[SLA example] On 2025-12-25 via app_chat, client wrote: "From where…" — no outbound response in 18 days.
